headline: Oil jumps +3–4% as Hormuz traffic stays near-stalled.
Shipping attacks continue as Hormuz effectively shut.
Hormuz remains effectively blocked; oil up ~4–6% on the day; Qatar LNG outage persists.
Hormuz still near-standstill (de facto disruption); oil slightly lower vs last check; Gulf states signal collective defense.
UKMTO: tug reportedly hit by unknown projectiles 6nm north of Oman (SoH).
Concerns over ship attacks persist; reports of more vessels turning back near Hormuz.
Risk premiums rise; limited traffic through Hormuz; more reports of projectile strikes and GPS interference.
Reports indicate continuing Iranian restrictions; shipping lines assess reroutes and convoy options.
Limited Gulf departures; insurers and charterers tighten war-risk terms amid continued threats in Hormuz approaches.
US and partners signal expanded naval coordination as attacks and interference persist.
Market focus on potential escalation; Hormuz transits remain far below normal.
Analysts warn of prolonged disruptions; operators report degraded AIS/GNSS near Hormuz approaches.
More warnings issued for shipping in Gulf and Hormuz; risk of mines and projectiles persists.
UKMTO: vessel hit by projectile near Hormuz; crew safe; authorities investigating.
Iran signals restrictions to ‘hostile’ shipping; more incidents logged in Gulf/Hormuz region.
War-risk costs surge as ship attacks and navigation interference persist near Hormuz.
Reports of controlled passage corridors emerge; most commercial operators still avoiding transits.
U.S. Maritime Administration issues advisory 2026-004 on Iranian attacks in Persian Gulf/Strait of Hormuz.
Military operations expand; some shipping begins using permission-based routing; transits still far below normal.
International efforts discussed for convoy or coalition protection as select vessels transit under heightened risk.
Navigation interference reported near Hormuz approaches; shipping queues build at regional anchorages.
Reports: Iran implements selective, permission-based transit regime; traffic remains sharply reduced.
Insurers adjust war-risk cover; some operators consider Oman corridors amid ongoing conflict.
Limited passage continues; official warnings urge vessels to avoid predictability and minimize time at anchor.
Market volatility persists on mixed signals about reopening and continued attacks in Gulf region.
No clear de-escalation; limited crossings; operators report ongoing spoofing/jamming.
Reports of intermittent transit permissions; GCC discusses international security framework.
Conflict-driven disruption continues; oil elevated; shipping insurance costs remain high.
International statements condemn de facto closure; reports of mines and drone threats persist.
More countries endorse statement condemning de facto closure; calls for multinational naval coalition intensify.
US issues ultimatum on reopening; deadline later extended to April 6.
Oil volatile as markets assess risk of escalation; limited shipping continues under selective controls.
Reports: Iran tightens permission-based routing; US and Israel target mine-laying capabilities.
Bahrain circulates draft UNSC resolution (Chapter VII) to secure transit passage; discussions ongoing.
Trump threatens to strike Iran’s power plants unless Hormuz fully reopened; deadline set to April 6.
JMIC: overall maritime risk level CRITICAL; threats persist; limited SoH traffic and continued interference.
No confirmed new attacks in prior 24h per advisory; disruption persists and traffic remains extremely low.
Advisories: SoH traffic observed at ~1 vessel/day (AIS-derived) on Mar 24–25; threat remains CRITICAL.
JMIC Update 024 (26 Mar): threat remains CRITICAL; observed SoH traffic ~1 vessel/day (AIS-derived) on Mar 24–25; 0 vessel attacks past 48h.
JMIC Update 025 (29 Mar): threat remains CRITICAL; 11 vessels observed in last 24h (AIS-derived); 0 vessel attacks past 72h.
CNBC: UN Security Council shipping-protection draft ("all necessary means") reportedly stalled; Iran continues curtailing tanker movements through Hormuz.

JMIC Update 025 (29 Mar): threat level remains CRITICAL; observed Strait of Hormuz traffic remains far below normal (~138/day), with 11 vessels observed in the last 24 hours (AIS-derived); no vessel attacks reported in past 72 hours.

Thailand: Fuel retailers raised pump prices Apr 3 (diesel +3.50 baht/litre; petrol/gasohol +0.70); exports +9.9% y/y in Feb but with widening trade deficit as imports +31.8%; Krungsri estimates Middle East escalation could cut Thailand GDP by 0.2–0.9pp via oil and logistics shocks.

JMIC Update 026 (31 Mar): Overall Maritime Risk Level remains CRITICAL; observed Strait of Hormuz traffic last 24h: 5 vessels vs historical ~138/day (AIS-derived); two incidents on 30 Mar (AL SALMI hit by unknown projectile with fire extinguished; EXPRESS ROME reported 2 missiles splashing 50–100m away).

UKMTO Warning 030-26 (31 Mar/1 Apr): tanker struck by two projectiles 17NM north of Ras Laffan, Qatar; one caused fire (extinguished) and another remained unexploded in engine room; crew safe; Hormuz disruption persists.

Thailand: Diesel prices raised again effective Apr 5 05:00 (standard diesel +2.80 baht/litre; some premium diesel +4.80 to +6.50) while petrol/gasohol unchanged, per OR/PTT and Bangchak retail price lists.
